creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
(In reply to MMacD from comment #0)
> After logging out to text mode in 10.3 RELEASE, and calling startx again,  X
> starts up with the twm window manager, not xfce4.  Twm provides only the
> mouse cursor, no terminal, so a deadstart is required to recover.

Umm.. So how are you (re)starting X?
For xfce(4), it's usually started via ~/.xinitrc
containing at least the entry:

startxfce4

When not using an xdm of some sort, ie; starting X automatically
at boot.

So unless you're [already] attempting it in this fashion.
This should not be considered a bug. :-)
BTW I'm not the MAINTAINER for x11-wm/xfce4. Just tryin' to help. :-)

--Chris
